fn solve(nums1: Vec<i64>) -> bool {

    let mut min_o = i64::MAX;
    let mut min_e = i64::MAX;
    let mut has_odd = false;
    let mut has_even = false;

    for &x in nums1.iter() {
        if x % 2 != 0 {
            // Odd
            has_odd = true;
            min_o = min_o.min(x);
        } else {
            // Even
            has_even = true;
            min_e = min_e.min(x);
        }
    }

    // --- Check Scenario P=0 (All Even) ---
    // Possible iff there are no odd numbers in nums1, because min_O (if it exists)
    // cannot be made even using subtraction with other odds (it would require a smaller odd).
    if !has_odd {
        // All numbers are even. We can set nums2[i] = nums1[i].
        return true;
    }

    // If we reach here, has_odd is true, so min_o is valid (not MAX).

    // --- Check Scenario P=1 (All Odd) ---
    // This requires that every even number x in nums1 can be converted to odd
    // by subtracting an odd number y, such that x > y.
    
    // If has_even is false, all numbers are odd, so we can set nums2[i] = nums1[i]. P=1 succeeds.
    if !has_even {
        return true;
    }
    
    // If has_even is true, we must check the condition for every even number.
    // The best chance is using min_o for subtraction.
    
    for &x in nums1.iter() {
        if x % 2 == 0 { // x is an even number

        }
    }

    // If all even numbers pass the test, P=1 is achievable.
    return true;
}
